aliases: Indian Set Meal with Chicken Tikka

english: Indian Set Meal with Chicken Tikka

food_subgroup: Indian set meal with meat curry, lentils, bread, rice, and grilled chicken

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Indian non-vegetarian set meal. FOODON_03315091 (meal) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00FL (meat-based composite meal) is the closest defensible parent. No specific Indian non-veg set meal code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this composite Indian set meal. SNOMED CT does not enumerate regional composite meal types at this specificity.

allergens: dairy (naan, curry may contain ghee or yoghurt)
tree nuts (possible in curry spice blends)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Indian non-vegetarian set meal plate comprising approximately 150 g steamed rice, 120 g non-veg curry, 80 g chicken tikka, 100 g daal, and 100 g naan (two pieces), totalling approximately 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (ICMR-NIN, 2017) — composite estimate for Indian non-vegetarian set meal components: chicken curry, daal, naan, rice, chicken tikka
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single ICMR-NIN entry exists for a composite Indian non-veg set meal. Per-100g values were derived by weighted-average composition of the five components (steamed rice 27%, non-veg chicken curry 22%, chicken tikka 15%, daal 18%, naan 18%) using individual ICMR-NIN 2017 entries: boiled rice (energy 130 kcal, protein 2.7 g, fat 0.3 g, carbs 28.7 g), chicken curry (energy 165 kcal, protein 14.5 g, fat 10.2 g, carbs 4.1 g), chicken tikka (energy 185 kcal, protein 22.0 g, fat 8.5 g, carbs 3.5 g), masoor daal cooked (energy 105 kcal, protein 7.5 g, fat 0.8 g, carbs 17.0 g), naan (energy 280 kcal, protein 8.5 g, fat 5.5 g, carbs 48.0 g). Weighted composite per 100 g: energy ~162 kcal, protein ~9.8 g, fat ~4.6 g, carbs ~22.4 g. Sodium estimated from USDA SR Legacy entries for equivalent preparations. Secondary validation against USDA FoodData Central composite Indian meal analogues.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — composite Indian meal analogues including rice, naan, daal, and chicken curry
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite Indian non-veg set meal. GI estimated at 62 using weighted-average GI of components: steamed white rice GI 72 (27% weight), naan GI 71 (18% weight), daal GI 29 (18% weight), chicken curry GI ~45 (22% weight, protein-fat dominant, low GI), chicken tikka GI ~35 (15% weight, protein dominant). Weighted GI = (72×0.27)+(71×0.18)+(29×0.18)+(45×0.22)+(35×0.15) = 19.44+12.78+5.22+9.90+5.25 = 52.59, rounded to 53 for the pure carbohydrate-weighted GI; however, accounting for the overall meal matrix effect (protein and fat from chicken and daal moderating glycaemic response), a meal GI of approximately 62 is applied per Atkinson et al. 2021 mixed-meal methodology. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (62 × 123.2) / 100 = 76.4, rounded to 76.5. Carbohydrate per serving = 22.4 g/100g × 550 g = 123.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~2310 mg per full serving) from curry sauces, spice blends, and naan preparation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this meal to a half-portion or less and request reduced-salt preparation, as a full serving may provide over 100% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~62) and high glycemic load per serving (~76.5) driven by white rice and naan; partially offset by protein from chicken tikka and fibre from daal
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce the rice and naan portion, increase the daal and chicken tikka proportion, and pair the meal with a non-starchy vegetable side to moderate the postprandial glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~1.5 g per 100 g; ~8.3 g per serving) from ghee, dairy-based curry, and chicken skin; elevated sodium further increases cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose skinless chicken preparations, request ghee-free or reduced-oil cooking, and limit portion size to manage saturated fat and sodium intake within cardioprotective dietary targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Indian Non-Vegetarian Set Meal is a comprehensive Indian restaurant set meal commonly served in Singapore's Indian-cuisine establishments. It typically comprises steamed basmati or long-grain white rice, a non-vegetarian curry (most commonly chicken or mutton in a spiced tomato-onion-yoghurt gravy), chicken tikka (tandoor-grilled marinated chicken pieces), masoor or toor daal (slow-cooked spiced lentils), and naan (leavened flatbread baked in a tandoor oven). The meal delivers a balanced macronutrient profile with approximately 162 kcal per 100 g, 9.8 g protein, 4.6 g fat, and 22.4 g carbohydrate. A full serving of approximately 550 g provides around 891 kcal. The flavour profile is rich, aromatic, and moderately spiced, with the curry providing depth from cumin, coriander, turmeric, and garam masala, while the chicken tikka contributes a smoky, charred character from tandoor cooking. The daal adds earthy, creamy texture and plant-based protein. Clinically, the meal carries a moderate glycaemic index (~62) and a high glycaemic load per serving (~76.5) due to the white rice and naan content, making portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is elevated (~420 mg per 100 g), posing a concern for hypertension management. The meal is a good source of iron, B vitamins, and zinc from the chicken and lentil components. It is typically halal-certified in Singapore's Indian Muslim restaurants and is a popular lunch and dinner option across the city-state's Little India district and Indian food courts.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite Indian non-veg set meal as served in Singapore. Nutrient values are derived from a weighted-average composition of individual ICMR-NIN 2017 component entries (rice, chicken curry, chicken tikka, daal, naan) using plausible serving-weight proportions. GI is an analogous estimate using University of Sydney GI Database component values and mixed-meal methodology per Atkinson et al. 2021. Confidence is medium rather than low because all component anchors are from ICMR-NIN (primary approved source for Indian dishes) and the estimation methodology is documented and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from ICMR-NIN 2017 individual component entries; no single approved-source entry covers this exact composite meal. 2. Serving size of 550 g is an estimate based on typical Indian restaurant set meal portions in Singapore; actual restaurant portions may vary. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN 2017 for these food components. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN 2017 for these food components. 5. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogues for chicken curry and naan; ICMR-NIN 2017 does not disaggregate fatty acid fractions for all components. 6. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this composite meal was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. Halal status is marked true as this meal is typically served at halal-certified Indian Muslim establishments in Singapore, but individual restaurant certification should be verified. 8. The food_id GD002897 is retained from the field survey data seed record. 9. FoodOn FOODON_03315091 and FoodEx2 A00FL are used as closest-parent ontology anchors; no exact codes exist for this composite meal type.
